article_title: Iranian Drone Strikes on AWS Data Centers Mark the First Wartime Attack on Hyperscale Cloud Infrastructure

content_preview.title: Iranian Drone Strikes on AWS Data Centers Mark the First Wartime Attack on Hyperscale Cloud Infrastructure
content_preview.summary: Iranian drones struck three AWS facilities in the UAE and Bahrain, knocking two availability zones offline and challenging the cloud industry's core resilience assumptions.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

In the early hours of March 1, 2026, Iranian drones struck two Amazon Web Services data centers in the United Arab Emirates and damaged a third facility in Bahrain, according to [The Register](https://www.theregister.com/2026/03/02/amazon_outages_middle_east/). The attacks knocked two of the three availability zones in AWS's ME-CENTRAL-1 region offline and disrupted services in Bahrain's ME-SOUTH-1 region, marking what analysts believe to be the first confirmed military strike on hy...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong analysis article on unprecedented wartime attack on cloud infra. Well-structured.
editor_notes.source_verification: Fortune confirmed: March 1 strikes, first wartime hyperscale attack, dual-use military/civilian concern, AI strategic role. CBS News confirmed: 3 facilities, structural damage, power disruption, water damage, Amazon stock impact, customer advisory.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All core claims verified across 2 major sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral analytical tone appropriate for Analysis category.
editor_notes.originality: No prior AWS drone strikes coverage. Novel topic.
